businessSummary.oneLiner: World's leading EV maker and Physical AI platform spanning autonomous driving, robotaxi, humanoid robots, energy storage, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is expanding beyond EV sales into full autonomous driving (FSD), Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage systems. The company reported annual revenue of $94.8B in 2025 and GAAP EPS of $1.08, while achieving gross margins of 20.1% in Q4 2025—the highest in two years. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue, with April 2026 marked by two critical catalysts: Cybercab mass production launch and FSD European regulatory approval.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and solar generation (Megapack, Powerwall, solar systems)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA software updates)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Trading at 15x the S&P 500 average (22x) and 27x higher than auto industry average (12x). Extreme premium reflects market expectations for EV, AI, and robotics growth

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026 consensus EPS of $1.43. Earnings recovery expectations are priced in, yet valuation remains in the stratosphere

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 EPS Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: 60% increase vs. Q1 2025 ($0.15). However, Tesla has missed consensus for four consecutive quarters—credibility is at risk

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Full-year 2025 revenue declined. Q1 2026 deliveries of 358,023 units missed consensus of 365,000. Recovery delayed concerns mounting

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: 2025 baseline. Q4 reached 20.1%, showing improvement trend. Trump tariffs pose additional margin compression risk

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Up 73.7% year-over-year. Cash generation significantly improved, yet P/FCF ratio of 198x indicates continued overvaluation

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab mass production begins April, robotaxi narrative reignited
growthDrivers.description: Fully autonomous, steering-wheel-free Cybercab production starts in April at Texas Gigafactory. First unit produced in February, 25 units in March, now undergoing crash testing. Confirmation of accelerating production ramp will reignite robotaxi hype

growthDrivers.title: FSD European approval decision April 10, global expansion crossroads
growthDrivers.description: Netherlands transportation authority (RDW) announces FSD approval decision on April 10. Tesla has completed 1.6 million km road testing and 13,000 customer test rides across EU roads. Approval would trigger spike in FSD subscribers and establish foundation for Cybercab European rollout

growthDrivers.title: Q1 2026 earnings April 22, consensus EPS $0.24
growthDrivers.description: Q1 2026 results announced April 22. Consensus EPS $0.24 represents 60% growth versus Q1 2025. However, four consecutive quarters of missed guidance makes achievement uncertain and will be critical to near-term stock direction

growthDrivers.title: FSD v12.3 released, full autonomy within reach
growthDrivers.description: FSD v12.3 processes city and highway driving entirely with neural networks. $99/month subscription model expanding recurring revenue. FSD v14 European launch hints appeared in software update 2026.8.6

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Vertical integration in manufacturing, autonomous driving data flywheel, and charging infrastructure create a three-part moat dominating EV market while preparing for AI robotics era

moats.description: 4680 battery cells and Gigafactory automation drive continuous cost reduction. Per-unit margins 2-3x higher than competitors
6 billion accumulated miles of real-world driving data. FSD improvement velocity vastly outpaces competition
50,000 Superchargers controls 80% of North America market share. Ford and GM now adopting NACS standard, increasing dependence on Tesla
Dojo supercomputer, FSD chips, Optimus robots—vertical hardware-software integration unmatched in industry

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's EV #1 by volume, pricing advantage. However, autonomous driving technology lags Tesla by 2-3 years

competitors.name: Ford, GM
competitors.detail: Legacy automakers transitioning to EV, expanding losses. Now adopting Tesla's charging standard, deepening dependence

competitors.name: Waymo, Cruise
competitors.detail: Robotaxi pioneers but geographically limited. Tesla's FSD enables global expansion capabilities

recentNews.headline: JPMorgan downgrade warning: Tesla stock could fall another 60%
recentNews.significance: JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman cites slowing deliveries, declining profit, record-high inventory to maintain Underweight rating and issue sharp decline warning

recentNews.headline: Wall Street consensus: Tesla price target $416.15, implies 22% upside
recentNews.significance: Among 43 analysts, 15 recommend strong buy, 16 hold, 10 strong sell. Extreme divergence of opinion continues

recentNews.headline: Q1 2026 preview: EPS $0.24 consensus, four consecutive quarters of misses
recentNews.significance: April 22 earnings will be crucial. Achievement of $0.24 uncertain due to tariff impact on margins

recentNews.headline: FSD v14 European launch hints detected in software update 2026.8.6
recentNews.significance: Software update reveals preparation signals for European FSD v14 rollout. Raises anticipation ahead of April 10 RDW approval decision

recentNews.headline: Q1 2026 deliveries 358,023 units, misses consensus of 365,000
recentNews.significance: 14% decline from prior quarter. Timing with Trump tariff announcement triggered 5% stock decline. Earnings concerns intensify

analystOpinions.notableComment: JPMorgan warns of 60% potential downside, while consensus target of $416 implies 20% upside. Tariff shock and earnings concerns create extreme analyst divergence

risks.title: Trump tariff shock
risks.description: Massive tariff announcement on April 2 triggered broad market selloff. Impact of tariffs on Tesla's global supply chain (Texas, Shanghai, Berlin, Mexico) remains unquantified, perpetuating uncertainty

risks.title: Q1 earnings miss risk
risks.description: Q1 deliveries of 358,023 units missed consensus. Four consecutive quarters of EPS misses. April 22 earnings miss on $0.24 consensus combined with high P/E will trigger sharp decline

risks.title: JPMorgan 60% downside warning
risks.description: JPMorgan cites slowing deliveries, declining profit, record inventory to maintain Underweight. Powerful sell signal pressuring market psychology

risks.title: FSD European approval uncertainty
risks.description: RDW rejection on April 10 would damage global expansion narrative. Two prior delays raise risk of additional postponement

buyReasons.title: FSD European approval imminent (4/10)
buyReasons.rationale: Netherlands RDW decision arrives tomorrow. Approval triggers spike in European FSD subscribers and establishes foundation for Cybercab European launch

buyReasons.title: Cybercab production acceleration
buyReasons.rationale: Texas Gigafactory begins mass production in April. Robotaxi commercialization becomes real, tapping $100B+ global taxi market TAM

buyReasons.title: RSI 33 approaches oversold
buyReasons.rationale: RSI 33.48 nears traditional 30 oversold threshold. Technical rebound conditions forming

buyReasons.title: Q1 earnings recovery potential
buyReasons.rationale: Consensus EPS $0.24 represents 60% year-over-year growth. Achievement would trigger rebound despite macro uncertainty

overallOpinion: Trump tariff shock and Q1 delivery disappointment drove stock from $361 to $348 over five days, a 3.7% decline reflecting broader market anxiety
Tomorrow (April 10) Dutch authorities announce FSD European approval—approval could trigger rebound while rejection creates additional downside pressure
April 22 Q1 earnings announcing EPS $0.24 will be the critical test. Tesla has missed consensus for four straight quarters, so expectations should be lowered

Stock trades below the 200-day moving average at $397 with RSI 33 approaching oversold conditions, but tariff and earnings risks remain unresolved. Rebound potential is limited until both events clear

investmentVerdict.summary: Powerful growth narrative around FSD European approval and Cybercab mass production exists, but Trump tariff shock and Q1 earnings weakness hit simultaneously. RSI near oversold creates technical rebound conditions, but it's prudent to wait for FSD approval result on April 10 and earnings on April 22 before repositioning

x.hook: FSD European approval D-1 amid tariff storm
x.text:
$TSLA (4/9) Tariff shock hammered stock to $348. FSD European approval decision comes tomorrow. Approval means rebound, rejection means more downside. $348.03.

1/4
thread:
Q1 deliveries 358,023 units, missed consensus 365,000. JPMorgan warns 60% further decline. April 22 EPS $0.24 earnings is the real turning point.

2/4

Cybercab rolling off Texas Gigafactory production line in April. FSD v14 European software ready. The narrative is still intact.

3/4

RSI 33, oversold approaching. Consensus target $416 (+20%). Watch tomorrow's RDW decision for direction, then reassess after April 22 earnings.

4/4

threads.hook: FSD European approval D-1, Tesla at inflection point amid tariff storm
threads.text:
$TSLA (4/9) $348.03 (-3.7% weekly)

Trump tariff announcement shocked markets. Q1 deliveries also disappointed at 358,023 units versus consensus 365,000. Tesla stock fell from $361 to $348 over five days, reflecting broader market unease.

Tomorrow (April 10) is critical. Netherlands transportation authority (RDW) announces FSD European approval decision. Two prior delays mean this is the third attempt. Tesla submitted 1.6 million km of EU road test data. Approval unlocks European FSD subscriber surge and Cybercab European expansion platform. Rejection adds downside pressure.

April 22 Q1 earnings announcing $0.24 EPS is the real test. Tesla has missed consensus for four straight quarters, so maintain low expectations. JPMorgan warns 60% downside while consensus target stands at $416 offering 20% upside. Both events must clear before repositioning. Technical oversold conditions exist (RSI 33) but risk unresolved.

Consensus target: $416 (+20%)

witty.hook: Company building steering-wheel-free robotaxi but stock has lost the wheel
witty.text:
$TSLA (4/9) The company building fully autonomous Cybercab without a steering wheel, yet its stock has lost the wheel entirely and drifted to $348. FSD European approval decision arrives tomorrow.

1/4
thread:
Trump tariffs and Q1 delivery miss (358,023 vs 365,000 consensus expected) collided at once. JPMorgan says another 60% drop possible. Still a long road ahead.

2/4

Yet Cybercab trucks are rolling in Texas. FSD v14 European software version standing by. The narrative remains very much alive.

3/4

RSI 33, oversold near. Target $416 (+20%). One word from RDW tomorrow could flip sentiment. This company never gets boring.

4/4

witty.hook: Company building wheel-free robotaxi while its stock wheels are spinning freely
witty.text:
$TSLA (4/9) $348.03

The irony is stark. Tesla builds a fully autonomous Cybercab without a steering wheel while the stock itself has lost control and drifted to $348. Trump tariffs collided with Q1 delivery miss (358,023 units, 7,000 short of expected). JPMorgan warns 60% further decline. Yet tomorrow (April 10) Netherlands RDW announces FSD European approval decision. This is the third attempt after two prior delays. This company never allows boredom.

RSI 33, oversold territory. Consensus target $416 (20% upside). April 22 Q1 earnings is the real test. Tesla has missed consensus four straight quarters, so temper expectations. But one word from RDW tomorrow could shift sentiment entirely. The story adapts faster than competitors can react.

empathy.hook: Tariffs and delivery misses collide, Tesla holders in genuine pain
empathy.text:
$TSLA (4/9) $348.03

If you own Tesla, you're having a rough week. Trump tariff shock hit, then Q1 deliveries disappointed at 358,023 units against 365,000 consensus. The stock fell from $361 to $348 in five days alone. JPMorgan warns 60% more downside possible. The fear is real and justified.

Yet tomorrow matters. April 10 brings the Netherlands RDW decision on FSD European approval. Two prior delays, but this time it's finally happening. Tesla provided 1.6 million km of EU road test data and 13,000 customer test rides. Approval means European FSD subscriber surge and Cybercab European expansion foundation.

Two weeks remain until April 22 Q1 earnings. Tesla has missed consensus for four quarters running, so set expectations low. RSI 33 means oversold conditions. Consensus target $416 is 20% higher. Wait for both events to clear before deciding. You've held this long—two more weeks won't hurt.
